name: Eirene
description: Eirene, also known as Pax in Roman mythology, is the Greek goddess of peace. She is one of the Horae, the goddesses of the seasons and the natural order. Eirene is the daughter of Zeus, the king of the gods, and Themis, the personification of divine law and order. As the goddess of peace, Eirene represents prosperity, wealth, and springtime. Her presence signifies the end of conflict and the beginning of harmony. She is often depicted as a beautiful young woman holding a cornucopia (horn of plenty), a scepter, or an olive branch, all symbols of abundance and peace. Eirene's worship was particularly significant in Athens, where a statue of her holding the infant Plutus (god of wealth) was erected in the Agora. This statue, sculpted by Cephisodotus the Elder, symbolized the connection between peace and prosperity. Eirene's influence extends to the realms of diplomacy, trade, and agriculture, as these activities flourish best in times of tranquility. She embodies the ideal of a stable and flourishing society where citizens can thrive without the disruptions of war. Her role as one of the Horae further emphasizes her connection to the natural cycles and the orderly progression of time, highlighting peace as an essential element of a balanced and harmonious world. Eirene's power lies not in brute force, but in her ability to inspire cooperation, understanding, and reconciliation among individuals and nations. She is a reminder of the importance of peace as a foundation for a prosperous and fulfilling life.
subtitle: Goddess of Peace and Prosperity

keyMyths.title: Eirene's Statue in Athens
keyMyths.description: A statue of Eirene holding the infant Plutus was erected in the Athenian Agora, symbolizing the connection between peace and wealth. This highlights the importance of peace for economic prosperity.

keyMyths.title: Eirene as a Horae
keyMyths.description: As one of the Horae, Eirene represents the natural order and the cyclical nature of time, highlighting peace as a vital component of a balanced world. She ensures the orderly progression of the seasons.

keyMyths.title: Eirene's Role in Society
keyMyths.description: Eirene's presence promotes diplomacy, trade, and agriculture, fostering a stable and flourishing society where citizens can thrive without the disruptions of war. She embodies the ideal of tranquility.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides information on Eirene's parentage and role as a Horae.
